categories.label: CIF Issue Items (Must Be CIF Issue)

items.name: Fighting Load Carrier (FLC)
items.description: CIF issue FLC. Name tape will be sewn on the rear drag handle.
items.notes: Name Tape will be sewn on the rear drag handle of the FLC.
items.whyNeeded: Primary load-bearing vest used throughout all field operations in SUT.

items.name: Canteen, 1 QT
items.description: CIF issue one-quart canteen.

items.name: Case, First Aid / IFAK
items.description: CIF issue individual first aid kit carrier.
items.whyNeeded: Required medical kit case for all field operations.

items.name: Case, Small Arms / Double M4 Mag Pouch
items.description: CIF issue double M4 magazine pouch.

items.name: Compass, Magnetic with Case
items.description: CIF issue lensatic compass with carrying case.
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is a core SUT skill. Two compasses required in case one fails.

items.name: Cover, Canteen 1 QT
items.description: CIF issue cover for one-quart canteen.

items.name: Cup, Canteen
items.description: CIF issue canteen cup.

items.name: MOLLE 4K Rucksack System, OCP Pattern
items.description: CIF issue MOLLE 4K rucksack in OCP pattern. Nametape and cat eyes must be sewn on top flap.
items.notes: Nametape and cat eyes must be sewn on top flap. MOLLE 4K rucksack MUST be brought as a mandatory item even if a commercial ruck is also brought. NSNs: System 8465-01-673-3374; Right shoulder strap 8465-01-673-3510; Left shoulder strap 8465-01-673-3517; Frame 8465-01-673-3400; Waist belt 8465-01-673-3449.
items.whyNeeded: Primary rucksack for SUT. The MOLLE 4K must be present even if an additional commercial ruck is authorized.
items.selectionTips: Sew nametape and cat eyes on the top flap before arriving. An additional military-style commercial rucksack may be used at cadre's discretion but cannot replace this item.

items.name: Canteen, 2 QT
items.description: CIF issue two-quart canteen.

items.name: Cover, Canteen 2 QT
items.description: CIF issue cover for two-quart canteen.

items.name: Carrier, Entrenching Tool
items.description: CIF issue e-tool carrier.

items.name: Entrenching Tool (E-Tool)
items.description: CIF issue folding shovel (entrenching tool).
items.whyNeeded: Required for field operations — fighting positions, field craft.

items.name: Bag, Barracks (Laundry Bag)
items.description: CIF issue barracks/laundry bag.

items.name: Bag, Waterproof
items.description: CIF issue waterproof bag for protecting gear and clothing.
items.whyNeeded: Keeps clothing and sensitive gear dry during wet weather operations.

items.name: Stuff Sack
items.description: CIF issue stuff sack.

items.name: Sleep Set — Inner Bag
items.description: CIF issue sleep set inner (summer/lightweight) bag.
items.whyNeeded: Required sleep system component.

items.name: Sleep Set — Outer Bag
items.description: CIF issue sleep set outer (winter) bag. See Note 7 — required October 1 through March 30.
items.notes: See Note 7. Cold weather gear required 1 October through 30 March.
items.whyNeeded: Required sleep system component for winter operations.

items.name: Sleeping Mat / Pad
items.description: CIF issue sleeping mat or pad.
items.whyNeeded: Insulation from ground during field sleep.

items.name: GORE-TEX Bivy Cover
items.description: CIF issue GORE-TEX bivy cover for sleeping bag system.
items.whyNeeded: Required sleep system component. Protects sleeping bag from rain and moisture.

items.name: Wet Weather Jacket (Level VI / ECWS Parka)
items.description: CIF issue ECWS wet weather parka (Level VI). See Note 3 — mandatory for winter classes.
items.notes: CIF issued ECWS parka is MANDATORY during WINTER classes (1 Oct–30 Mar). Army PCU level 5/6 combined may substitute during winter classes.
items.whyNeeded: Required for rain protection during field operations.

items.name: Wet Weather Trousers (Level VI / ECWS)
items.description: CIF issue ECWS wet weather trousers (Level VI). See Note 3 — mandatory for winter classes.
items.notes: CIF issued ECWS trousers are MANDATORY during WINTER classes (1 Oct–30 Mar). Army PCU level 5/6 combined may substitute during winter classes.
items.whyNeeded: Required for rain protection during field operations.

items.name: Soft Shell Cold Weather Jacket (Level V)
items.description: CIF issue soft shell cold weather jacket (Level V / PCU). See Note 3.
items.notes: Required 1 October through 30 March (winter classes).
items.whyNeeded: Insulation layer for cold weather operations.

items.name: Soft Shell Cold Weather Trousers (Level V)
items.description: CIF issue soft shell cold weather trousers (Level V / PCU). See Note 3.
items.notes: Required 1 October through 30 March (winter classes).
items.whyNeeded: Insulation layer for cold weather operations.

items.name: Helmet, ACH with OCP Cover
items.description: CIF issue Advanced Combat Helmet (ACH) with OCP cover. Must include chinstrap assembly and foam impact pads x7 (1x Crown, 2x trapezoid, 4x oval). Impact pads must be ACU colored.
items.notes: Must have: OCP cover, chinstrap assembly, and 7 foam impact pads (1x Crown pad, 2x trapezoid pads, 4x oval pads). Impact pads must be ACU colored pads.
items.whyNeeded: Required for all live fire and weapons ranges.
items.selectionTips: Ensure pads are the correct ACU color and all 7 pads are present before arriving.

items.name: Poncho (Camouflage/ACU/OCP) or CIF Small Tarp
items.description: CIF issue camouflage/ACU/OCP poncho or CIF issued small tarp.
items.whyNeeded: Field shelter and rain protection. Used to build hasty shelters.

items.name: OCP Patrol Cap
items.description: OCP patrol cap with luminous tape and nametag sewn on. Luminous cat eyes sewn above nametape.
items.notes: All students will arrive with luminous tape and nametag sewn on headgear. Cat eyes sewn above nametape on patrol cap.
items.selectionTips: Sew on luminous cat eyes above the sewn-on nametape before arriving. See page 6 of the packing list for diagram.

items.name: OCP Blouse (Coat)
items.description: OCP blouse (coat). Multi-Cam/OCP standard issue only. No Patagonia/CRYE, no Combat Shirts. Minimum rank, name tag, and US Army on each uniform.
items.notes: 4 sets MINIMUM — includes what is worn. No Patagonia/CRYE uniforms or 'Combat Shirts' authorized. Must have rank, name tag, and US Army on each uniform. Additional skill badges may be worn at soldier's discretion.
items.selectionTips: Standard Multi-Cam/OCP only. No knee pad slots or extra pockets (Crye-style).

items.name: OCP Trouser
items.description: OCP trousers. Minimum 4 sets. Knee pads NOT authorized for wear during the course.
items.notes: 4 sets MINIMUM — includes what is worn. KNEE PADS NOT authorized for wear, including pads concealed inside the knee patch of the trouser.
items.selectionTips: No knee pads of any kind — including pads sewn into knee patches.

items.name: Duffel Bag or Aviator Kit Bag with Padlock
items.description: Military issue duffel bag or aviator kit bag with padlock.
items.notes: No student will be allowed to load the truck with more than 1 rucksack and 1 kit/duffle bag.

items.name: Belt, Tan/Brown Web with Buckle
items.description: Military issue tan or brown web belt with buckle.

items.name: Boots
items.description: AR 670-1 compliant boots. Minimum 2 pairs. No alterations except sole replacement (waffle soles). Hot Weather and/or Intermediate Cold Boots (200g insulation) authorized.
items.notes: 2 pairs MINIMUM. Only AR 670-1 compliant boots authorized for wear. Boots may not be altered except for sole replacement (waffle soles). One set of AR 670-1 approved cold weather boots required 1 October through 30 March (Winter classes). No GORE-TEX boots.
items.whyNeeded: Foot health is critical to making it through SUT.
items.selectionTips: Break in all pairs well in advance. One pair should be hot weather, one intermediate cold (for winter classes 1 Oct–30 Mar).

items.name: CAC / ID Card
items.description: Common Access Card (CAC) or military ID card. Must be valid through the entire training period.
items.notes: Must be valid through end of training. Validate on an Army network computer prior to arrival.
items.whyNeeded: Required for access to facilities and verification.
items.selectionTips: Verify your CAC is valid and working on an Army network computer before arriving.

items.name: Cold Weather Underwear (Army Issue)
items.description: Army issue cold weather base layer underwear (Poly-Pro or Gen III). See Note 7 — required October 1 through March 30.
items.notes: 2 SETS MINIMUM. Army issue only. Cold weather gear required 1 October through 30 March.
items.whyNeeded: Required cold weather base layer for winter operations.

items.name: Ear Plugs with Case
items.description: Ear plugs with carrying case.
items.notes: 2 sets.
items.whyNeeded: Required for all live fire and weapons qualification events.

items.name: Eye Protection (APEL — Clear Lens)
items.description: Army Authorized Protective Eyewear (APEL), clear lens only. Must meet APEL ballistic validation requirements.
items.notes: CLEAR ballistic eye protection that meets APEL validation requirements. Clear lens only.
items.whyNeeded: Required for all ranges and field operations.
items.selectionTips: Clear lens only. No tinted lenses.

items.name: Prescription Eyeglasses (Military) with Elastic Retainer
items.description: Military prescription eyeglasses with elastic retainer bands. Required for personnel with a military prescription — contact lenses will NOT be worn.
items.notes: Required for personnel with a military prescription. Contact lenses will NOT be worn at any time during the course. 2 sets.
items.selectionTips: Contact lenses are prohibited. Wear military-issue glasses with elastic retainer at all times.

items.name: Gloves, Leather
items.description: Military issue leather gloves.
items.notes: 2 pairs.

items.name: ID Tags (Dog Tags) with Breakaway Chain
items.description: Military dog tags with breakaway chain. One set worn while in OCPs.
items.notes: 2 sets. 1 worn while in OCPs.
items.whyNeeded: Mandatory identification worn at all times during field operations.

items.name: Medical Alert Tags or Bracelet
items.description: Medical alert identification if applicable.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: IPFU Uniform (Complete)
items.description: Improved Physical Fitness Uniform — complete set: 2x shorts, 2x shirts, long pants, jacket.
items.notes: Complete set: 2x Shorts, 2x Shirts, Long Pants, Jacket.
items.whyNeeded: Required for PT events.

items.name: Reflective Belt
items.description: Standard reflective safety belt.
items.whyNeeded: Required for road running and low-light PT.

items.name: Wool Socks (Green, Grey, Coyote, or Black)
items.description: Wool socks in authorized colors: green, grey, coyote, or black.
items.notes: 6 pairs MINIMUM. Green, Grey, Coyote, or Black only.
items.whyNeeded: Wool socks are critical for foot health during extended field operations.
items.selectionTips: Bring extras. Wet feet are the enemy — have enough dry socks to rotate.

items.name: T-Shirt
items.description: Military issue T-shirts. Compression type T-shirts are prohibited.
items.notes: 6 total. Compression type T-shirts are NOT authorized.
items.selectionTips: No compression shirts. Standard military T-shirts only.

items.name: Watch Cap with Sewn Cat Eyes (for Field Use)
items.description: Military watch cap. Cat eyes sewn on for field use. Required October 1 through March 30.
items.notes: Cat eyes sewn on for field use. Required 1 October through 30 March (winter classes).
items.whyNeeded: Cold weather head protection. Cat eyes required for night identification.
items.selectionTips: Sew cat eyes onto the watch cap before arriving.

items.name: 3-Day Army Assault MOLLE II Backpack
items.description: Military issue 3-day assault pack (MOLLE II).
items.whyNeeded: Day pack for shorter operations and assault missions.

categories.label: Additional Mandatory Items (Military or Civilian Authorized)

items.name: Cash
items.description: Up to $100 maximum. Students will NOT be authorized use of the shopette during the course.
items.notes: $100 MAXIMUM. Students are not authorized use of the shopette. Bring all required toiletries to last the full duration.
items.whyNeeded: For any incidental needs. Shopette access is NOT authorized.
items.selectionTips: Bring up to $100 max.

items.name: CamelBak / Hydration System
items.description: Personal hydration carrier as required.
items.notes: As required.
items.whyNeeded: Hydration is critical during extended field operations.

items.name: Debit Card
items.description: Personal debit card.

items.name: Foot Powder, Anti-Fungal
items.description: Anti-fungal foot powder.
items.notes: 1 bottle.
items.whyNeeded: Prevents fungal infections and blisters during extended foot movement.

items.name: Gloves, Cold Weather
items.description: Cold weather gloves in subdued colors. Military or civilian authorized. Required October 1 through March 30.
items.notes: Subdued colors only. Required 1 October through 30 March (winter classes).
items.whyNeeded: Required for cold weather operations.

items.name: Headlamp with Red Filter and Spare Batteries
items.description: Headlamp with red filter capability and spare batteries included.
items.notes: Must have red filter. Bring spare batteries — B Co does not issue batteries for personal equipment.
items.whyNeeded: Red light required for night operations to preserve night vision.
items.selectionTips: Ensure it has a red filter mode. Bring plenty of spare batteries.

items.name: Insect Repellent / Chigg-Away
items.description: Insect repellent. Military or civilian. No aerosol cans.
items.notes: 2 bottles. No aerosol cans. Military or civilian authorized.
items.whyNeeded: Camp Mackall area has significant insect activity including chiggers.
items.selectionTips: Non-aerosol only. Chigg-Away is highly recommended for chigger protection.

items.name: Laundry Soap (Liquid Only)
items.description: Liquid laundry soap only. New high efficiency washers at Camp Mackall require liquid detergent.
items.notes: LIQUID ONLY. High efficiency washers at Camp Mackall. No pods or powder.
items.selectionTips: Liquid only — the washers at CMK are HE (high efficiency).

items.name: Linen for Bunks / Pillow
items.description: Bed sheets and pillow for barracks bunks.
items.notes: As required.

items.name: Notebook with Pen and Pencil
items.description: Standard notebook with pen and pencil. SUT does NOT provide supplies including pens, paper, etc.
items.notes: As required. SUT does NOT provide personal supplies including batteries, tape, pens, or paper.
items.whyNeeded: Used for instruction and note-taking throughout the course.
items.selectionTips: Bring enough for the duration. No resupply available.

items.name: Padlock or Combination Lock
items.description: Padlock or combination lock for securing gear.

items.name: Razors with Blades (Non-Electric)
items.description: Non-electric razors with blades. Part of 6-week personal hygiene kit.
items.notes: Minimum 10. Non-electric only. Part of 6-week supply hygiene kit. Students will NOT have shopette access.
items.whyNeeded: Grooming standards must be maintained throughout the course.
items.selectionTips: Bring enough for 6 weeks with no resupply.

items.name: Toothbrush with Toothpaste
items.description: Toothbrush and toothpaste. 6-week supply. Part of personal hygiene kit.
items.notes: Minimum 2 toothbrushes. 6-week supply of toothpaste.

items.name: Soap (Anti-Bacterial)
items.description: Anti-bacterial soap. 6-week supply. Part of personal hygiene kit.
items.notes: Anti-bacterial. As required for 6-week duration.

items.name: Shaving Cream
items.description: Shaving cream. 6-week supply. Part of personal hygiene kit.
items.notes: As required for 6-week duration.

items.name: Chapstick / Lip Balm
items.description: Lip balm. Part of personal hygiene kit.
items.notes: As required.

items.name: Prescription Medication
items.description: Prescription medications as needed. Must notify cadre during layout.
items.notes: NOTIFY CADRE DURING LAYOUT. As needed.
items.selectionTips: Disclose all prescription medications to cadre during the initial layout.

items.name: Running Shoes
items.description: Running shoes for PT.
items.whyNeeded: Required for physical training events.

items.name: Shower Shoes
items.description: Flip-flops or shower shoes.
items.whyNeeded: Required for field hygiene facilities.

items.name: Towels
items.description: Standard bath towels.

items.name: PT Socks (White or Black, No Stripes)
items.description: PT socks in white or black. No stripes.
items.notes: 4 pairs. White or black only. No stripes.

items.name: Whistle
items.description: Standard safety whistle.
items.whyNeeded: Used for signaling during field operations.

items.name: Wristwatch
items.description: Durable and dependable wristwatch. No GPS or wireless data transfer capability.
items.notes: No GPS or wireless data transfer capable wristwatches.
items.whyNeeded: Time management is critical during all tactical operations.
items.selectionTips: Simple, durable watch. No smart features, no GPS.

items.name: Aiming Stakes
items.description: 18 inches long, 1 inch or less in diameter, wood or plastic, rigid.
items.notes: 18" long, 1 inch or less diameter, wood or plastic, rigid.

items.name: Alarm Clock
items.description: Small wind-up or battery alarm clock. No clock radios.
items.notes: No clock radios.

items.name: Alcohol Pens and Erasers
items.description: Alcohol-based markers and erasers for use on map cases and overlays.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Baby Wipes, Antibacterial
items.description: Antibacterial baby wipes for field hygiene.
items.notes: As needed.
items.whyNeeded: Field hygiene when shower access is limited.

items.name: Batteries
items.description: Extra batteries for personal equipment. B Co does NOT issue batteries for personal gear.
items.notes: As needed. B Co does NOT issue batteries for personal equipment.
items.selectionTips: Bring enough for the full duration. No resupply available.

items.name: Bible or Religious Writings
items.description: Bible or other approved religious writings.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Professional Military Books / Handbooks
items.description: SUT Handbook or other professional military guides/manuals. Maximum 2 books.
items.notes: 2 MAX. Hard copy only. Cadre will have final determination on appropriateness. Materials deemed inappropriate will be collected, tagged, and secured.

items.name: OCP Boonie / Jungle Cap
items.description: OCP boonie or jungle cap. Must have cat eyes sewn on if worn.
items.notes: OCP Boonie Cap must have cat eyes sewn on if worn.
items.selectionTips: Sew cat eyes on if you plan to wear it.

items.name: Intermediate Cold Weather Boots
items.description: Intermediate cold weather boots (200g insulation), AR 670-1 compliant.
items.notes: See Note 1. AR 670-1 compliant. Insulated 200 grams. Recommended for winter classes (1 Oct–30 Mar).
items.whyNeeded: Provides additional warmth during winter field operations.

items.name: Bungee Cords
items.description: Bungee cords for securing gear.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Butt Pack (for M240 Ammo 7.62mm Link)
items.description: Butt pack for carrying M240 machine gun ammunition (7.62mm link).
items.notes: For M240 ammo 7.62mm link.

items.name: Camouflage Face Paint Kit with Mirror
items.description: Military face paint camouflage kit with mirror.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Cold Weather Clothing (Additional)
items.description: Additional cold weather clothing beyond the mandatory items.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Chewing Gum (Non-Caffeinated)
items.description: Non-caffeinated chewing gum.
items.notes: Non-caffeinated only.

items.name: Cell Phone with Charger
items.description: Cell phone with charger. Will be secured/stored by Cadre on Day 1.
items.notes: WILL BE SECURED/STORED BY CADRE ON DAY 1. Cameras, voice recorders, and media storage devices are prohibited.
items.selectionTips: Your phone will be taken and secured by cadre on the first day.

items.name: Field Jacket Liner ('Smoking Jacket')
items.description: Coat liner for field jacket.

items.name: Dump Pouch (Highly Recommended)
items.description: Drop pouch for spent magazines. Highly recommended.
items.notes: Highly Recommended.
items.whyNeeded: Essential for managing magazines during live fire and dry-fire drills.
items.selectionTips: Highly recommended — speeds up mag changes significantly.

items.name: Flashlight with Spare Batteries
items.description: Handheld flashlight with spare batteries.
items.notes: Bring spare batteries.

items.name: Tactical Shooting Gloves (Leather Palms)
items.description: Tactical gloves with leather palms.
items.notes: 2 pairs. Leather palms.

items.name: Hair Trimmer, Electric
items.description: Electric hair trimmer for haircuts.
items.notes: Students must arrive with a fresh haircut IAW AR 670-1. No barber/haircut facilities at Camp Mackall.
items.whyNeeded: No barber facilities at Camp Mackall. Students must maintain appearance throughout.
items.selectionTips: Arrive with a fresh haircut. Bring a trimmer to maintain it.

items.name: Knife (Max Blade 7 inches)
items.description: Knife with maximum blade length of 7 inches. Folding/pocket knife preferred.
items.notes: Maximum blade length 7 inches. Folding/pocket knife preferred.

items.name: Lighter or Fire Starting Device
items.description: Lighter or other fire starting device.

items.name: Boot Laces, Combat
items.description: Spare combat boot laces.
items.notes: 1 pair.

items.name: Leatherman Multi-Tool (Highly Recommended)
items.description: Leatherman or similar multi-tool. Highly recommended.
items.notes: Highly Recommended.
items.whyNeeded: Versatile tool for field repairs and maintenance.

items.name: M-4 Front Sight Post Adjustor
items.description: Tool for adjusting M-4 front sight post.

items.name: Machine Gun Cleaning Tools
items.description: M249 Combination Regulator Scraper Tool and/or M240 Reamers.
items.notes: M249 Combination Regulator Scraper Tool and M240 Reamers. As needed.

items.name: Map Case (Subdued)
items.description: Subdued color map case.
items.notes: Subdued colors only.
items.whyNeeded: Protects maps during field operations.

items.name: Dry Erase Marker
items.description: Dry erase marker for map overlays.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: MOS Reference Materials (Hard Copy Only)
items.description: MOS-specific reference materials. Hard copy only — no digital.
items.notes: Hard copy only. No digital/electronic references.

items.name: Nalgene Bottle
items.description: Nalgene or similar hard-sided water bottle.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: NVG Head Mount 'Crye Cap' with Rhino Mount
items.description: NVG head mount (Crye-style cap) with Rhino mount. NOT available for issue by SUT — bring your own.
items.notes: Rhino mount required. NOT available from SUT — must be privately purchased.

items.name: Pace Count Cord (Ranger Beads)
items.description: 100-meter pace count cord for land navigation.

items.name: Peltor Ear Muffs / Hearing Protection (Subdued)
items.description: Peltor or equivalent hearing protection in subdued color.
items.notes: Subdued color only.
items.whyNeeded: Additional hearing protection for sustained fire ranges.

items.name: Postal Materials (Envelopes, Stamps)
items.description: Envelopes, stamps, and postal materials.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Poncho Liner (Woobie) — Highly Recommended
items.description: Military poncho liner. Highly recommended.
items.notes: Highly Recommended.
items.whyNeeded: Versatile warmth layer that can supplement the sleep system or be used as a standalone blanket.
items.selectionTips: Highly recommended — one of the most useful items in the field.

items.name: MOLLE Pouches (Assorted)
items.description: Assorted pouches for MOLLE rucksack and FLC.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Protractor
items.description: Military protractor for land navigation and map reading.
items.whyNeeded: Used for plotting grid coordinates and measuring azimuths.

items.name: Q-Tips / Cotton Tip Applicators (Weapons Cleaning)
items.description: Cotton tip applicators for weapons cleaning.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Rain Cover, Rucksack (OCP Pattern Only)
items.description: OCP pattern rucksack rain cover. OCP pattern only — other patterns unauthorized.
items.notes: OCP pattern only authorized.
items.whyNeeded: Protects rucksack contents from rain during field operations.

items.name: Ranger Handbook (April 2017)
items.description: Army Ranger Handbook, April 2017 edition. Available for issue by cadre.
items.notes: April 2017 edition. May be available for issue by cadre.

items.name: Commercial / Aftermarket Rucksack
items.description: Additional military-style commercial rucksack at cadre's discretion. Cannot replace the MOLLE 4K.
items.notes: At cadre discretion. MOLLE 4K rucksack must still be brought as mandatory item — this is supplemental only.
items.selectionTips: Cannot replace the mandatory MOLLE 4K. Bring it in addition to the MOLLE 4K.

items.name: SAW Pouches
items.description: Squad Automatic Weapon (SAW) pouches for M249 ammunition.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Sewing Kit
items.description: Small sewing kit for field repairs.

items.name: Signal Mirror
items.description: Military signal mirror.

items.name: Snaplink, Locking
items.description: Locking carabiner / snaplink.

items.name: Sleep Shirt
items.description: Comfortable sleep shirt for barracks.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Speed Loaders (M-4 / M-9)
items.description: Magazine speed loaders for M-4 and/or M-9.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Sunscreen (Non-Aerosol)
items.description: Cream, lotion, or pump spray sunscreen. No aerosol sprays.
items.notes: Cream, lotion, or pump spray only. No aerosol sprays.

items.name: Terrain Model Kit
items.description: Kit for building terrain models during operations orders.

items.name: Tobacco Products
items.description: Tobacco products. No refillable e-cigarette or vape devices.
items.notes: NO refillable E-cig/Vape devices authorized. Bring enough to last the course — shopette access not authorized.
items.selectionTips: Bring a sufficient supply to last the full course. Shopette access will not be authorized.

items.name: Trucker Mic / Additional Handsets for Military Radios
items.description: Speaker/microphone or additional handsets for military radios.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Underwear (White or Brown)
items.description: Additional underwear in white or brown.
items.notes: White or brown only. As needed.

items.name: Washcloth
items.description: Standard washcloth.

items.name: Weapons Cleaning Kit
items.description: Standard weapons cleaning kit and supplies.
items.notes: As needed.
items.whyNeeded: Weapons must be kept clean and serviceable throughout the course.

items.name: Wrist Compass
items.description: Wrist-mounted compass for quick azimuth reference.

items.name: Zip Lock Bags
items.description: Zip lock bags for waterproofing documents and small items.
items.notes: As needed.

items.name: Female Urinary Diversion Device / FUDD (Female Students)
items.description: Female urinary diversion device. FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Mandatory for female candidates.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. MANDATORY — minimum 2. Listed here because female items are in a separate section.

items.name: Feminine Hygiene Products (Female Students)
items.description: Feminine hygiene products as required. FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Mandatory for female candidates.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. MANDATORY — as required.

items.name: Feminine Wipes (Female Students)
items.description: Feminine wipes as needed. FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Mandatory for female candidates.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. MANDATORY — as needed.

items.name: Sports Bra, No Wire/Hook Clasps (Female Students)
items.description: Sports bra with no wires or hook clasps. Subdued or natural colors (white, black, tan, brown). FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Mandatory for female candidates.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. MANDATORY — minimum 6. No wires, no hook clasps. Subdued or natural colors: white, black, tan, or brown.

items.name: Cotton Underwear (Female Students)
items.description: Cotton underwear in subdued or natural colors. FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Mandatory for female candidates.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. MANDATORY — minimum 6. Subdued or natural colors: white, black, tan, or brown.

items.name: Birth Control (Female Students — Optional)
items.description: Approved birth control with current prescription. Notify cadre during layout. FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY.
items.notes: FEMALE STUDENTS ONLY. Must have current prescription. Notify Cadre during layout.

items.name: Alcoholic Beverages
items.description: Alcohol of any kind is prohibited.

items.name: Compression Type T-Shirts
items.description: Compression T-shirts (Under Armour, etc.) are prohibited.
items.notes: Standard military T-shirts only.

items.name: MP-3 Players, iPods, and Media Players
items.description: Personal music/media players are prohibited.

items.name: Cameras and Voice Recorders
items.description: Cameras and voice recorders of any kind are prohibited.
items.notes: ANY media storage devices are prohibited.

items.name: Civilian GPS Units
items.description: No civilian GPS units of any kind. Students are issued the DAGR (Defense Advanced GPS Receiver).
items.notes: Student will be issued the 'Defense Advanced GPS Receiver' (DAGR) for use in SUT.

items.name: Contact Lenses
items.description: Contact lenses will NOT be worn at any time during the course.
items.notes: Military prescription eyeglasses with elastic retainer must be worn instead.

items.name: Knee Pads
items.description: Knee pads are NOT authorized for wear during the course — including pads concealed inside the knee patch of the uniform trouser.
items.notes: Includes pads concealed inside the knee patch of the OCP trouser.

items.name: Patagonia / CRYE Uniforms or Combat Shirts
items.description: No Patagonia/CRYE uniforms or 'Combat Shirts' of any kind.
items.notes: Multi-Cam/OCP standard issue only.

items.name: Refillable E-Cigarettes / Vape Devices
items.description: No refillable e-cig or vape devices authorized.
items.notes: Tobacco products (non-refillable) are on the optional authorized list.

items.name: Any Item Not on the Authorized Lists
items.description: Possession of any unauthorized item is a Violation of the Student Honor Code and will result in immediate RELIEF from the Small Unit Tactics Phase of the SFQC.
items.notes: Students will be checked and equipment inventoried for packing list compliance prior to beginning training.
